mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year rolling windows, or positive 20d returns exceed 60% during rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that outweigh demand, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes during tightening cycles yield positive 5d returns in > 60% of cases, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio < 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index rises above 195.0, inflation expectations accelerate, prompting market repricing of equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d returns are positive in > 58% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a stable unemployment rate signals a healthy labor market that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in a 60d return regression over a 4-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth reflects strong economic momentum that boosts equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth predicting 5d returns falls below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows, or positive return frequency drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 115.0 indicates a severe supply contraction that triggers risk-off sentiment, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances when INDPRO drops below 115.0, or the signal loses predictive power (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.
